qa_pairs.question: What is the procedure for listing the available images within your OpenStack project?
qa_pairs.answer: To list available images, you must first install the OpenStack client and then download and source the OpenStack RC file. Following these steps, you can execute the command `openstack image list`.

qa_pairs.question: How do you download a specific image from an OpenStack project to your local machine?
qa_pairs.answer: After setting up the OpenStack client and sourcing the OpenStack RC file, you can download a particular image using the command `openstack image save --file ./<file-name-for-image>.<format> <ID>`. Ensure that `<format>` matches the value in the 'Disk Format' column and `<ID>` corresponds to the image's ID.

qa_pairs.question: What is the command to upload a local image file to an OpenStack project, and what parameters are required?
qa_pairs.answer: To upload an image, first install the OpenStack client and source the OpenStack RC file. Then, run the command `openstack image create --file <path-to-local-file-image> --disk-format <format> <new-image-name>`. You must provide the path to your local image file, specify its disk format (otherwise, raw is assumed, which can cause issues), and assign a new name for the image as it will appear on the OpenStack dashboard.
